title: CLAUDE.md — cfbseedR Development Guide
text: cfbseedR simulates evaluates college football seasons: standings documented tiebreaker cascade, conference champions, College Football Playoff (CFP) straight seeding, week--week season simulation pluggable results generator. adaptation nflseedR (MIT, Lee Sharpe & Sebastian Carl) — credit DESCRIPTION/README/vignettes touching attribution. License: MIT. Site: https://cfbseedR.sportsdataverse.org Branch: main default release branch.

title: CLAUDE.md — cfbseedR Development Guide
text: Internal engine helpers live R/standings_utils.R (mirrors nflseedR’s standings_utils.R/standings_init.R architecture).

title: CLAUDE.md — cfbseedR Development Guide
text: Conference-REG-scoped sov/sos: tiebreaker cascade quantities (head--head, common opponents, SOV, SOS, point differential) computed regular-season conference games . sov conference victories, sos conference opponents; independents get 0.0. widen full-schedule scope. CONF_CHAMP semantics: conference championship games count toward overall record decide conf_champ, count toward conference record/rank. CONF_CHAMP matchups simulated scheduled, never re-derived simulated standings. Straight seeding (2025): 5 highest-ranked conference champions guaranteed inclusion; seeds assigned strictly ranking order — champions bumped top 4.

title: CLAUDE.md — cfbseedR Development Guide
text: sportsdataverse-py’s CFB standings implementation (cfb_standings Python side) follows conference-REG-scoped sov/sos ruling CONF_CHAMP semantics. Changes cascade, scoping, CONF_CHAMP handling must stay reconcilable Python side — change one, flag .

title: CLAUDE.md — cfbseedR Development Guide
text: Tidyverse style: snake_case, 2-space indent, native pipe |> (%>%), .data pronoun dplyr verbs, cli::cli_abort() / cli::cli_inform() messaging (never stop()/message()). testthat edition 3; tests offline bundled toy fixtures (inst/extdata/toy_games.csv / toy_teams.csv). 142+ tests must pass. roxygen2 markdown; @return blocks carry column tables; simulation examples wrapped \donttest{}. Run devtools::document() — never hand-edit man/ NAMESPACE. Vignettes vignettes/ .Rbuildignored (pkgdown articles); must stay runnable offline (toy data inline; eval = FALSE cfbfastR network chunks). R CMD check must stay 0 errors / 0 warnings.

title: CLAUDE.md — cfbseedR Development Guide
text: Conventional Commits (feat:, fix:, docs(pkgdown):, ci(actions):, …); one logical change per commit; stage explicit paths. Never add AI co-author trailers (Claude/Copilot/etc.) commits PRs — human author sole attributable contributor. Regenerate README.md README.Rmd (devtools::build_readme() rmarkdown::render("README.Rmd")) — never edit README.md directly. pkgdown site deploys gh-pages via .github/workflows/pkgdown.yaml; verify locally pkgdown::build_site() pushing site changes.

code: # e.g. # devtools::test() # cfb_standings(games, teams, tiebreaker_depth = "POINTS", verbosity = "MAX")

title: Getting started with cfbseedR
text: cfb_standings() needs games frame (sim/season, game_type, week, home_team, away_team, result) teams frame (team, conference). returns one row per team overall conference records, conference ranks (via tiebreaker cascade), conference champions. Two college-football-specific semantics aware : Conference championship games (game_type == "CONF_CHAMP") count toward overall record decide conference champion, toward conference record/rank. sov / sos conference-scoped: strength victory strength schedule computed regular-season conference games (sov conference victories, sos conference opponents); independents get 0.0. real data, cfb_games_from_schedule() maps cfbfastR::load_cfb_schedules() frame schema:
code: standings <- cfb_standings(games, teams, verbosity = "NONE") standings[, c("team", "conference", "wins", "losses", "conf_pct",               "conf_rank", "conf_champ")] #> # A tibble: 9 × 7 #>   team  conference        wins losses conf_pct conf_rank conf_champ #>   <chr> <chr>            <int>  <int>    <dbl>     <int> <lgl>      #> 1 A1    Alpha                3      2    0.667         1 TRUE       #> 2 A2    Alpha                2      2    0.667         2 FALSE      #> 3 A3    Alpha                2      1    0.667         3 FALSE      #> 4 A4    Alpha                0      4    0             4 FALSE      #> 5 B1    Beta                 5      0    1             1 TRUE       #> 6 B2    Beta                 2      2    0.667         2 FALSE      #> 7 B3    Beta                 1      2    0.333         3 FALSE      #> 8 B4    Beta                 0      4    0             4 FALSE      #> 9 I1    FBS Independents     2      0    0            NA FALSE # Not run: requires network access via cfbfastR sched <- cfbfastR::load_cfb_schedules(2024) games_2024 <- cfb_games_from_schedule(sched)

what: Playoff seeds with a rankings frame
title: Getting started with cfbseedR
text: cfb_playoff_seeds() implements CFP straight seeding (2025 rule): field best-ranked teams 5 highest-ranked conference champions guaranteed inclusion, seeded strictly ranking order. Pass committee-style rankings frame (team, rank); without one, documented fallback ordering (win pct, SOV, SOS, point differential) used.
code: rankings <- data.frame(team = c("B1", "I1", "A1", "A3"), rank = 1:4) seeded <- cfb_playoff_seeds(standings, rankings = rankings, playoff_seeds = 4) seeded[!is.na(seeded$seed), c("team", "conference", "conf_champ", "seed")] #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   team  conference       conf_champ  seed #>   <chr> <chr>            <lgl>      <int> #> 1 A1    Alpha            TRUE           3 #> 2 A3    Alpha            FALSE          4 #> 3 B1    Beta             TRUE           1 #> 4 I1    FBS Independents FALSE          2

title: Getting started with cfbseedR
text: cfb_simulations() fills missing (NA) results week week using compute_results function - default cfbseedR_compute_results(), ELO-based generator adapted nflseedR - computes standings, champions, seeds, (sim_include = "POST") playoff bracket. returned cfbseedR_simulation list also carries per-simulation standings, simulated games, team_wins win-total probabilities, per-matchup game_summary, sim_params used. deeper topics - custom compute_results functions, tiebreaker_depth ladder, CFP-12 seeding rules - see Simulating seasons article.
code: games$result[games$week >= 3] <- NA set.seed(42) sim <- cfb_simulations(games, teams, simulations = 50, playoff_seeds = 4) #> Start simulation of 50 seasons (4 weeks to simulate). #> DONE! sim$overall #> # A tibble: 9 × 7 #>   conference       team   wins conf_champ playoff seed1 won_natty #>   <chr>            <chr> <dbl>      <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 Alpha            A1     3.26       0.88    0.9   0.02      0.34 #> 2 Alpha            A2     1.4        0.12    0.12  0.02      0.02 #> 3 Alpha            A3     2.72       0       0.88  0.7       0.06 #> 4 Alpha            A4     0.6        0       0     0         0    #> 5 Beta             B1     3.24       0.68    0.7   0.06      0.14 #> 6 Beta             B2     1.8        0.32    0.34  0         0.04 #> 7 Beta             B3     1.66       0       0.34  0         0.06 #> 8 Beta             B4     0.68       0       0.04  0         0    #> 9 FBS Independents I1     1.64       0       0.68  0.2       0.34

title: Getting started with cfbseedR
text: cfbseedR adaptation nflseedR (MIT) Lee Sharpe Sebastian Carl. entire architecture - standings engine, tiebreaking cascade design, week-loop simulator pluggable compute_results contract, ELO-based default generator - design, re-derived college football semantics. Thank Lee, Sebastian, broader nflverse team whose open-source work package builds .

title: Simulating seasons
text: cfb_simulations() calls compute_results(teams, games, week_num, ...) per remaining week. contract (identical nflseedR’s): return list(teams = teams, games = games), fill result exactly games week == week_num & .na(result), never modify result, remove rows/columns games, produce tie (result == 0) outside regular season. deliberately simple generator: every missing result home win 3 (postseason-safe since 3 never tie): simulations_verify_fct() checks custom function full contract burn simulation time : plug : Anything extra generator needs (ratings vector, market lines, externally trained model) can passed ... cfb_simulations(). default generator accepts initial ELO ratings way:
code: home_field_rules <- function(teams, games, week_num, ...) {   fill <- games$week == week_num & is.na(games$result)   games$result[fill] <- 3   list(teams = teams, games = games) } simulations_verify_fct(home_field_rules) set.seed(1) sim <- cfb_simulations(   games, teams,   compute_results = home_field_rules,   simulations = 10, playoff_seeds = 4 ) #> Start simulation of 10 seasons (4 weeks to simulate). #> DONE! sim$overall[, c("team", "wins", "conf_champ", "playoff")] #> # A tibble: 9 × 4 #>   team   wins conf_champ playoff #>   <chr> <dbl>      <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1 A1        4          1       1 #> 2 A2        2          0       1 #> 3 A3        2          0       1 #> 4 A4        0          0       0 #> 5 B1        4          1       1 #> 6 B2        2          0       0 #> 7 B3        1          0       0 #> 8 B4        1          0       0 #> 9 I1        1          0       0 elo <- setNames(seq(1700, 1300, length.out = nrow(teams)), teams$team) set.seed(2) sim_elo <- cfb_simulations(   games, teams,   simulations = 10, playoff_seeds = 4,   elo = elo ) #> Start simulation of 10 seasons (4 weeks to simulate). #> DONE! sim_elo$overall[, c("team", "wins", "playoff")] #> # A tibble: 9 × 3 #>   team   wins playoff #>   <chr> <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1 A1      2.9     0.4 #> 2 A2      2.1     0.6 #> 3 A3      2.5     1   #> 4 A4      0.6     0.1 #> 5 B1      3.6     0.6 #> 6 B2      2.1     0.7 #> 7 B3      1.2     0.1 #> 8 B4      0.8     0.1 #> 9 I1      1.2     0.4

title: Simulating seasons
text: Conference ranks seeded conference win percentage; ties broken cascade whose depth control: verbosity = "MAX" logs every step cascade takes, easiest way understand (trust) rank:
code: played <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "toy_games.csv", package = "cfbseedR")) standings <- cfb_standings(played, teams,                            tiebreaker_depth = "POINTS", verbosity = "MAX") #> Initiate standings & tiebreaking data #> Compute conference ranks #> Breaking tie of "A1", "A2", and "A3" in "Alpha" (sim 2024). standings[, c("team", "conference", "conf_pct", "sov", "sos", "conf_rank")] #> # A tibble: 9 × 6 #>   team  conference       conf_pct   sov   sos conf_rank #>   <chr> <chr>               <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>     <int> #> 1 A1    Alpha               0.667 0.333 0.444         1 #> 2 A2    Alpha               0.667 0.333 0.444         2 #> 3 A3    Alpha               0.667 0.333 0.444         3 #> 4 A4    Alpha               0     0     0.667         4 #> 5 B1    Beta                1     0.333 0.333         1 #> 6 B2    Beta                0.667 0.167 0.444         2 #> 7 B3    Beta                0.333 0     0.556         3 #> 8 B4    Beta                0     0     0.667         4 #> 9 I1    FBS Independents    0     0     0            NA

title: Simulating seasons
text: cascade quantities - head--head, common opponents, sov, sos, point differential - computed regular-season conference games , conference ranks depend conference play. Concretely: sov = beaten conference opponents’ conference wins divided conference games (strength victory conference victories), sos = conference opponents’ conference wins divided conference games (strength schedule conference opponents), independents conference games, 0.0. documented simplification: real CFB tiebreakers conference-specific, full-schedule SOS let non-conference results leak conference seeding.

title: Simulating seasons
text: generic cascade fallback used conferences without registered procedure. SEC, Big Ten, Big 12, ACC, MAC instead use official 2024+ tiebreaker procedures (ported registry sdv-py’s cfb_standings.py, engines agree shared cross-language fixture). add rungs generic cascade doesn’t - record vs. common opponents order finish, pooled conference opponents’ win percentage, SEC’s capped relative scoring margin (points scored capped 42 / allowed 48 per game), Big 12’s total_wins FCS--lower win cap, external analytics rating - always run completion (tiebreaker_depth gates generic fallback). Two optional inputs feed rungs available: home_points/away_points games (SEC cap) division column teams (Big 12 FCS cap); tiebreaker_data = list(analytics_ratings = ratings) supplies external rating rung. rung whose input missing skipped, skip recorded attr(standings, "tiebreak_notes"):
code: sec_teams <- data.frame(   team = c("A", "B", "C"), conference = "SEC", division = "FBS" ) sec_games <- data.frame(   sim = 2024, week = 1:3, game_type = "REG",   home_team = c("A", "B", "C"), away_team = c("B", "C", "A"),   result = c(50, 3, 3), neutral = 0,   home_points = c(50, 20, 20), away_points = c(0, 17, 17) ) sec_standings <- cfb_standings(sec_games, sec_teams,                                tiebreaker_depth = "POINTS", verbosity = "NONE") sec_standings[, c("team", "conf_rank")] #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   team  conf_rank #>   <chr>     <int> #> 1 A             1 #> 2 B             2 #> 3 C             3 attr(sec_standings, "tiebreak_notes") #> character(0)

title: Simulating seasons
text: cfb_playoff_seeds() implements 2025 straight seeding rule: Order teams committee rank (rankings); unranked teams fall back behind ranked ones (win pct, SOV, SOS, point differential rankings = NULL). 5 highest-ranked conference champions guaranteed spot (fewer fewer champions exist). Fill remaining spots best-ranked -large teams. Seed field strictly ranking order - champions bumped top-4 seeds (pre-2025 rule). synthetic 16-team example 6 conference champions shows guarantee straight ordering; champion F1 (ranked 14th) displaces lowest -large team keeps rank-order seed: cfb_simulations(), bracket generated seeds fixed CFP-12 bracket (first round hosted higher seed, later rounds neutral-site, reseeding), committee rankings held static across simulations.
code: st <- data.frame(   sim = 1,   team = sprintf("%s1", LETTERS[1:16]),   conference = c(LETTERS[1:6], LETTERS[1:10]),   conf_champ = c(rep(TRUE, 6), rep(FALSE, 10)),   win_pct = seq(1, 0.4, length.out = 16),   sov = 0.5, sos = 0.5, pd = 100 ) rankings <- data.frame(   team = st$team,   rank = c(1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 14, 4, 6, 7, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 15, 16) ) seeded <- cfb_playoff_seeds(st, rankings = rankings, playoff_seeds = 12) seeded[!is.na(seeded$seed), c("team", "conf_champ", "seed")] |>   (\(x) x[order(x$seed), ])() #> # A tibble: 12 × 3 #>    team  conf_champ  seed #>    <chr> <lgl>      <int> #>  1 A1    TRUE           1 #>  2 B1    TRUE           2 #>  3 C1    TRUE           3 #>  4 G1    FALSE          4 #>  5 D1    TRUE           5 #>  6 H1    FALSE          6 #>  7 I1    FALSE          7 #>  8 E1    TRUE           8 #>  9 J1    FALSE          9 #> 10 K1    FALSE         10 #> 11 L1    FALSE         11 #> 12 M1    FALSE         12

title: Simulating seasons
text: cfbfastR installed, real season plugs straight :
code: # Not run: requires network access via cfbfastR sched <- cfbfastR::load_cfb_schedules(2024) games_2024 <- cfb_games_from_schedule(sched) games_2024 <- games_2024[games_2024$game_type != "POST", ] teams_2024 <- unique(data.frame(   team = c(sched$home_team, sched$away_team),   conference = c(sched$home_conference, sched$away_conference) )) sim <- cfb_simulations(games_2024, teams_2024, simulations = 1000)

title: cfbseedR Copilot Instructions
text: cfbseedR R package simulates evaluates college football seasons: standings documented tiebreaker cascade, conference champions, College Football Playoff (CFP) straight seeding, week--week season simulation pluggable results generator. adapts nflseedR (MIT, Lee Sharpe & Sebastian Carl) college football semantics. Docs: https://cfbseedR.sportsdataverse.org

title: cfbseedR Copilot Instructions
text: cfb_standings(), cfb_playoff_seeds(), cfb_simulations(), cfbseedR_compute_results(), simulations_verify_fct(), cfb_games_from_schedule(). User-facing engine functions use cfb_ prefix; two helpers mirror nflseedR namesakes.

title: cfbseedR Copilot Instructions
text: sov/sos conference-REG-scoped: computed regular-season conference games ; sov conference victories, sos conference opponents; independents get 0.0. CONF_CHAMP games count toward overall record decide conf_champ, toward conference record/rank; matchups simulated scheduled. CFP straight seeding (2025): 5 highest-ranked conference champions guaranteed; seeds strictly ranking order (champion bump). rulings cross-validated sportsdataverse-py’s CFB standings implementation — keep two reconcilable.

title: cfbseedR • Simulate College Football Seasons
text: Functions efficiently simulate evaluate college football seasons: standings documented tiebreaker cascade, conference champions, College Football Playoff (CFP) seeding, week--week season simulation pluggable results generator. cfbseedR adapted nflseedR (MIT) Lee Sharpe Sebastian Carl nflverse, part SportsDataverse.

title: cfbseedR • Simulate College Football Seasons
text: can install development version cfbseedR GitHub :
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("sportsdataverse/cfbseedR")

title: cfbseedR • Simulate College Football Seasons
text: See Getting started article full walkthrough Simulating seasons custom compute_results functions, tiebreaker ladder, CFP-12 seeding rules.
code: library(cfbseedR)  # Bundled toy season (9 teams, 2 conferences + 1 independent) games <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "toy_games.csv", package = "cfbseedR")) teams <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "toy_teams.csv", package = "cfbseedR"))  # Standings with conference ranks and champions standings <- cfb_standings(games, teams, tiebreaker_depth = "POINTS")  # Simulate a season 100 times (blank results are simulated) games$result[games$week >= 3] <- NA set.seed(42) sim <- cfb_simulations(games, teams, simulations = 100, playoff_seeds = 4) sim$overall  # Real data via cfbfastR (Suggests; the engine itself needs no cfbfastR) # sched <- cfbfastR::load_cfb_schedules(2024) # games <- cfb_games_from_schedule(sched)

title: cfbseedR • Simulate College Football Seasons
text: Following SportsDataverse convention, engine’s user-facing exports prefixed cfb_: cfb_standings(), cfb_playoff_seeds(), cfb_simulations(), cfb_games_from_schedule(). Two helpers mirror nflseedR namesakes instead: cfbseedR_compute_results() (default results generator, nflseedR_compute_results()) simulations_verify_fct() (custom-generator contract checker).

title: cfbseedR • Simulate College Football Seasons
text: Conferences, divisions. Standings group conference; independents ("FBS Independents" NA conference) appear overall standings get conference rank. Conference championship games (game_type == "CONF_CHAMP") count toward overall record decide conference champion, count toward conference record/rank. CFP 12-team straight seeding (2025 rule) via cfb_playoff_seeds(): 5 highest-ranked conference champions guaranteed inclusion; field seeded strictly committee rank (rankings).

what: Documented simplifications vs. real CFB / nflseedR
title: cfbseedR • Simulate College Football Seasons
text: tiebreaker cascade generic (win pct → head--head → common opponents → SOV → SOS → point differential → coin flip), per-conference official tiebreak rules. cascade quantities computed regular-season conference games conference ranks depend conference play. sov/sos standings output conference-scoped (opponents’ conference records regular-season conference games; sov conference victories, sos conference opponents); independents get 0. rankings = NULL, cfb_playoff_seeds() falls back ordering teams win pct, SOV, SOS, point differential instead committee ranking. Committee rankings static across simulations. Conference championship matchups simulated scheduled — engine re-derive participants simulated standings. Playoff games generated input games game_type == "POST" rows; first round hosted higher seed, later rounds neutral-site. draft order (CFB concept). chunked/parallel simulation (nflseedR’s chunks/future support); simulations run sequentially.

title: cfbseedR • Simulate College Football Seasons
text: architecture (standings init → conference ranks → seeds; week-loop simulation pluggable compute_results; ELO-based default results generator) direct adaptation nflseedR (MIT), Copyright (c) Lee Sharpe Sebastian Carl. Huge thanks broader nflverse team original design.

what: Build an Engine Games Table from a cfbfastR Schedule — cfb_games_from_schedule
title: Build an Engine Games Table from a cfbfastR Schedule — cfb_games_from_schedule
text: Maps schedule shape returned cfbfastR::load_cfb_schedules() games schema used cfb_standings() cfb_simulations(). cfbfastR required - data frame expected columns works.

title: Build an Engine Games Table from a cfbfastR Schedule — cfb_games_from_schedule
code: cfb_games_from_schedule(schedule)

title: Build an Engine Games Table from a cfbfastR Schedule — cfb_games_from_schedule
text: schedule data frame least season, week, season_type, home_team, away_team. Optional columns used present: home_points/away_points (compute result), neutral_site, notes (games whose notes mention "championship" classified "CONF_CHAMP").

title: Build an Engine Games Table from a cfbfastR Schedule — cfb_games_from_schedule
text: tibble engine games schema, ready cfb_standings() / cfb_simulations():

title: Build an Engine Games Table from a cfbfastR Schedule — cfb_games_from_schedule
text: Game type mapping: season_type == "postseason" becomes "POST", games whose notes contain "championship" (case-insensitive) become "CONF_CHAMP", everything else "REG". documented heuristic - CFBD marks conference championship games regular-season games championship note.

title: Build an Engine Games Table from a cfbfastR Schedule — cfb_games_from_schedule
code: schedule <- data.frame(   season = 2024, week = c(1, 1, 15, 16),   season_type = c("regular", "regular", "regular", "postseason"),   home_team = c("A1", "B1", "A1", "A1"),   away_team = c("A2", "B2", "A2", "B1"),   home_points = c(21, 24, 17, NA), away_points = c(14, 20, 14, NA),   neutral_site = c(FALSE, FALSE, TRUE, TRUE),   notes = c(NA, NA, "Alpha Championship Game", NA) ) cfb_games_from_schedule(schedule) #> # A tibble: 4 × 7 #>   season  week game_type  home_team away_team result neutral #>    <dbl> <dbl> <chr>      <chr>     <chr>      <dbl>   <int> #> 1   2024     1 REG        A1        A2             7       0 #> 2   2024     1 REG        B1        B2             4       0 #> 3   2024    15 CONF_CHAMP A1        A2             3       1 #> 4   2024    16 POST       A1        B1            NA       1

what: Compute College Football Playoff Seeds — cfb_playoff_seeds
title: Compute College Football Playoff Seeds — cfb_playoff_seeds
text: Implements 12-team CFP straight seeding (2025 rule): field playoff_seeds best-ranked teams 5 highest-ranked conference champions guaranteed inclusion, seeds assigned strictly ranking order (champions bumped ).

title: Compute College Football Playoff Seeds — cfb_playoff_seeds
code: cfb_playoff_seeds(standings, rankings = NULL, playoff_seeds = 12L)

title: Compute College Football Playoff Seeds — cfb_playoff_seeds
text: standings standings table returned cfb_standings() (requires least team, conference, conf_champ, win_pct, sov, sos, pd, sim season id column). rankings Optional data frame columns team rank (1 = best), e.g. CFP committee rankings. Teams missing rankings treated unranked ordered behind ranked teams. NULL, documented fallback ordering used instead: teams ordered win percentage, strength victory, strength schedule, point differential, team name. playoff_seeds Number playoff spots (default 12).

title: Compute College Football Playoff Seeds — cfb_playoff_seeds
text: standings input (columns unchanged; see cfb_standings() column table) one column added:

title: Compute College Football Playoff Seeds — cfb_playoff_seeds
text: fewer 5 conference champions, champions guaranteed (capped playoff_seeds). guaranteed champion ranked outside top playoff_seeds displaces lowest-ranked -large team seeded rank order within field (.e. takes last seed).

title: Compute College Football Playoff Seeds — cfb_playoff_seeds
code: games <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "toy_games.csv", package = "cfbseedR")) teams <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "toy_teams.csv", package = "cfbseedR")) standings <- cfb_standings(games, teams, tiebreaker_depth = "POINTS",                            verbosity = "NONE") rankings <- data.frame(team = c("B1", "I1", "A1", "A3"), rank = 1:4) seeded <- cfb_playoff_seeds(standings, rankings = rankings, playoff_seeds = 4) seeded[!is.na(seeded$seed), c("team", "seed")] #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>   team   seed #>   <chr> <int> #> 1 A1        3 #> 2 A3        4 #> 3 B1        1 #> 4 I1        2

what: Simulate a College Football Season — cfb_simulations
title: Simulate a College Football Season — cfb_simulations
text: Simulates college football seasons based games/schedule table holds matchups without results. Missing results computed week week using pluggable compute_results function (default: cfbseedR_compute_results(), ELO-based generator adapted nflseedR). scheduled games, standings, conference champions, CFP seeds computed, - sim_include = "POST" - playoff bracket simulated round round.

title: Simulate a College Football Season — cfb_simulations
code: cfb_simulations(   games,   teams,   compute_results = cfbseedR_compute_results,   ...,   simulations = 10000L,   playoff_seeds = 12L,   tiebreaker_depth = c("SOS", "PRE-SOV", "POINTS", "RANDOM"),   sim_include = c("POST", "REG"),   rankings = NULL )

title: Simulate a College Football Season — cfb_simulations
text: games data frame games single season, schema cfb_standings() plus optional neutral column (0/1). Games result = NA simulated. Must contain game_type == "POST" rows - playoff bracket generated computed seeds. teams data frame columns team conference. Teams conference "FBS Independents" NA treated independents: appear overall standings receive conference rank. optional division column (e.g. "FBS"/"FCS") feeds Big 12 total_wins FCS cap; absent -> cap degrades uncapped win totals (noted, see tiebreak_notes ). teams need list every team appears games - unlisted opponent (e.g. FCS--lower team) gets standings row , games still count toward opponents' records toward Big 12 total_wins FCS cap (unknown opponent counts FCS--lower). compute_results function computing results games, required arguments teams, games, week_num. See simulations_verify_fct() contract cfbseedR_compute_results() default. ... Additional parameters passed compute_results. simulations number times season shall simulated. playoff_seeds Number CFP spots (default 12), passed cfb_playoff_seeds(). tiebreaker_depth One "SOS" (default), "PRE-SOV", "POINTS", "RANDOM". Controls deep tiebreaker cascade goes falling back coin flip: "RANDOM": coin flip immediately. "PRE-SOV": head--head common opponents . "SOS": adds strength victory, strength schedule. "POINTS": adds conference point differential. depth ladder gates generic fallback cascade used unregistered conferences; SEC/Big Ten/Big 12/ACC/MAC official procedures always run full. sim_include One "POST" (default) "REG": "REG": simulate remaining schedule compute standings, conference champions, playoff seeds. "POST": "REG" + simulate playoff bracket. rankings Optional committee rankings (team, rank) used CFP seeding, held static across simulations. NULL, seeding falls back per-simulation standings ordering (see cfb_playoff_seeds()).

title: Simulate a College Football Season — cfb_simulations
text: list class cfbseedR_simulation elements:

title: Simulate a College Football Season — cfb_simulations
text: playoff bracket standard single-elimination bracket size 2^ceiling(log2(playoff_seeds)) byes top seeds - 12 seeds reproduces CFP bracket (quarterfinals 1 vs 8/9 winner, 4 vs 5/12, 3 vs 6/11, 2 vs 7/10). First-round games hosted higher seed; later rounds neutral-site. reseeding (fixed bracket, per CFP format). Conference championship matchups simulated scheduled, re-derived simulated standings. Simulations run sequentially (chunk/parallel support). Set seed set.seed() reproducibility.

title: Simulate a College Football Season — cfb_simulations
code: # \donttest{ games <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "toy_games.csv", package = "cfbseedR")) teams <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "toy_teams.csv", package = "cfbseedR")) games$result[games$week >= 3] <- NA set.seed(4) sim <- cfb_simulations(games, teams, simulations = 4, playoff_seeds = 4) #> Start simulation of 4 seasons (4 weeks to simulate). #> DONE! sim$overall #> # A tibble: 9 × 7 #>   conference       team   wins conf_champ playoff seed1 won_natty #>   <chr>            <chr> <dbl>      <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 Alpha            A1     2.75       0.5     0.75  0         0    #> 2 Alpha            A2     1.75       0.5     0.5   0.25      0    #> 3 Alpha            A3     2.75       0       1     0.5       0.25 #> 4 Alpha            A4     1          0       0.25  0         0    #> 5 Beta             B1     4          0.75    0.75  0.25      0.5  #> 6 Beta             B2     2.25       0.25    0.25  0         0    #> 7 Beta             B3     1          0       0     0         0    #> 8 Beta             B4     0          0       0     0         0    #> 9 FBS Independents I1     1.5        0       0.5   0         0.25 # }

title: Compute College Football Standings — cfb_standings
text: Computes overall conference standings table game results, including conference ranks (via documented tiebreaker cascade), conference champions, - optionally - College Football Playoff seeds. Adapted nflseedR's nfl_standings() college football semantics: conferences instead divisions, independents excluded conference ranks, conference championship games counting toward overall record (deciding champion) conference record.

title: Compute College Football Standings — cfb_standings
code: cfb_standings(   games,   teams,   ...,   tiebreaker_depth = c("SOS", "PRE-SOV", "POINTS", "RANDOM"),   playoff_seeds = NULL,   rankings = NULL,   tiebreaker_data = NULL,   verbosity = c("MIN", "MAX", "NONE") )

title: Compute College Football Standings — cfb_standings
text: games data frame games. Required columns: sim season season simulation ID. game_type One "REG", "CONF_CHAMP", "POST". week Week number game. home_team, away_team Team names matching teams$team. result Home margin, .e. home score minus away score. Must NA (play simulate games first). Optional home_points/away_points columns (per-game scores) feed official SEC capped_scoring_margin tiebreaker rung (see "Official per-conference tiebreakers" ); cfb_games_from_schedule() emits . Absent -> rung skipped, error. teams data frame columns team conference. Teams conference "FBS Independents" NA treated independents: appear overall standings receive conference rank. optional division column (e.g. "FBS"/"FCS") feeds Big 12 total_wins FCS cap; absent -> cap degrades uncapped win totals (noted, see tiebreak_notes ). teams need list every team appears games - unlisted opponent (e.g. FCS--lower team) gets standings row , games still count toward opponents' records toward Big 12 total_wins FCS cap (unknown opponent counts FCS--lower). ... Currently unused. tiebreaker_depth One "SOS" (default), "PRE-SOV", "POINTS", "RANDOM". Controls deep tiebreaker cascade goes falling back coin flip: "RANDOM": coin flip immediately. "PRE-SOV": head--head common opponents . "SOS": adds strength victory, strength schedule. "POINTS": adds conference point differential. depth ladder gates generic fallback cascade used unregistered conferences; SEC/Big Ten/Big 12/ACC/MAC official procedures always run full. playoff_seeds NULL, seed column added via cfb_playoff_seeds() number playoff spots. rankings Optional committee-style rankings data frame columns team rank, passed cfb_playoff_seeds(). Ignored playoff_seeds NULL. tiebreaker_data Optional named list external inputs official registry rungs. Currently supported: analytics_ratings, data frame columns team rating (feeds SportSource Analytics-style rating rung used Big Ten/Big 12/ACC/MAC). Missing -> rung skipped conferences (noted). verbosity One "MIN" (default), "MAX", "NONE". "MAX" logs every tied group broken.

title: Compute College Football Standings — cfb_standings
text: tibble standings, one row per (sim, team) (id column named season input used season), sorted sim, conference, conference rank, team. Note sov sos conference-REG-scoped: computed regular-season conference games , sov conference victories sos conference opponents; independents get 0.0 . result also carries character vector attr(result, "tiebreak_notes") recording registry rungs skipped lack optional input (see "Official per-conference tiebreakers" ); empty nothing skipped.

title: Compute College Football Standings — cfb_standings
text: Conference ranks seeded conference win percentage; ties within tier broken documented cascade. Registered conferences (SEC, Big Ten, Big 12, ACC, MAC) use official 2024+ procedures (see "Official per-conference tiebreakers" ); every conference uses generic fallback: head--head record among tied teams, record vs. common conference opponents (minimum one), conference-scoped strength victory, conference-scoped strength schedule, conference point differential, finally coin flip, gated tiebreaker_depth. cascade quantities computed regular-season conference games conference ranks depend conference play.

title: Compute College Football Standings — cfb_standings
text: CONFERENCE_TIEBREAKERS (internal) registers SEC, Big Ten, Big 12, ACC, MAC 2024+ procedures rung lists, ported verbatim sdv-py's cfb_standings.py engines produce identical output shared cross-language parity fixture. Rung primitives: h2h (multi-team combined head--head, applied every tied pair played; otherwise "defeated-" elimination - symmetric "lost--" elimination intentionally modeled, documented simplification, see R/tiebreakers.R), record_vs_common, record_vs_common_desc (descend standings best worst, comparing tied GROUP common opponents collectively - Big 12 rule, adopted every registry descent rung), opp_conf_win_pct (pooled opponents' conference win pct - reuses existing sos column, already computes pooled sum--wins/sum--games formula), capped_scoring_margin (SEC: points scored capped 42 / allowed capped 48, per game, summed conference games; needs home_points/away_points), total_wins (Big 12: overall wins one win vs FCS--lower opponent counted; needs teams$division), analytics_rating (external, via tiebreaker_data$analytics_ratings), coin_toss. team seeded/eliminated procedure restarts first rung remaining tied set; rung's required input unavailable skipped deterministically skip recorded (per conference) attr(result, "tiebreak_notes"). registry conferences, conf_rank 1-2 two teams reach conference championship game (cascade orders - see design brief).

title: Compute College Football Standings — cfb_standings
code: games <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "toy_games.csv", package = "cfbseedR")) teams <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "toy_teams.csv", package = "cfbseedR")) standings <- cfb_standings(games, teams, tiebreaker_depth = "POINTS",                            verbosity = "NONE") standings[, c("team", "conference", "conf_rank", "conf_champ")] #> # A tibble: 9 × 4 #>   team  conference       conf_rank conf_champ #>   <chr> <chr>                <int> <lgl>      #> 1 A1    Alpha                    1 TRUE       #> 2 A2    Alpha                    2 FALSE      #> 3 A3    Alpha                    3 FALSE      #> 4 A4    Alpha                    4 FALSE      #> 5 B1    Beta                     1 TRUE       #> 6 B2    Beta                     2 FALSE      #> 7 B3    Beta                     3 FALSE      #> 8 B4    Beta                     4 FALSE      #> 9 I1    FBS Independents        NA FALSE       # An official-registry analytics rating input (used by Big Ten/Big # 12/ACC/MAC when their cascade reaches the `analytics_rating` rung) ratings <- data.frame(team = teams$team, rating = seq(90, 70, length.out = nrow(teams))) standings2 <- cfb_standings(games, teams,                             tiebreaker_data = list(analytics_ratings = ratings),                             verbosity = "NONE") attr(standings2, "tiebreak_notes") #> character(0)

what: cfbseedR: Functions to Efficiently Simulate and Evaluate College Football Seasons — cfbseedR-package
title: cfbseedR: Functions to Efficiently Simulate and Evaluate College Football Seasons — cfbseedR-package
text: set functions simulate college football seasons compute standings, conference ranks, conference champions, College Football Playoff seeds. package adapts approach 'nflseedR' package Sebastian Carl Lee Sharpe (MIT licensed) college football semantics: conferences instead divisions, independents, conference championship games, 12-team playoff straight seeding.

title: cfbseedR: Functions to Efficiently Simulate and Evaluate College Football Seasons — cfbseedR-package
text: Maintainer: Saiem Gilani saiem.gilani@gmail.com (ORCID) Authors: Sebastian Carl mrcaseb@gmail.com (Author nflseedR, cfbseedR adapted) Lee Sharpe (Author nflseedR, cfbseedR adapted)

what: Compute CFB Game Results in Season Simulations — cfbseedR_compute_results
title: Compute CFB Game Results in Season Simulations — cfbseedR_compute_results
text: default compute_results function used cfb_simulations(). faithful adaptation nflseedR's nflseedR_compute_results() (variant 538's ELO model, originally coded Lee Sharpe rewritten Sebastian Carl): simple dynamic ELO carried teams table, updated week (real simulated) results.

title: Compute CFB Game Results in Season Simulations — cfbseedR_compute_results
code: cfbseedR_compute_results(teams, games, week_num, ...)

title: Compute CFB Game Results in Season Simulations — cfbseedR_compute_results
text: teams data frame teams simulation least columns sim team. May carry elo column weeks. games data frame games least sim, game_type, week, home_team, away_team, result, optionally neutral (0/1; home-field ELO bonus 20 applies neutral). week_num (numeric) week simulate. games week == week_num & .na(result) receive simulated result. ... Optionally pass elo, named vector initial ELO ratings (names = team names). absent, teams start random ratings drawn rnorm(1500, 150).

title: Compute CFB Game Results in Season Simulations — cfbseedR_compute_results
text: list two elements, required compute_results contract (see simulations_verify_fct()):

title: Compute CFB Game Results in Season Simulations — cfbseedR_compute_results
text: Mechanics (constants identical nflseedR): ELO difference home - away plus 20 true home games, multiplied 1.2 postseason (game_type != "REG"). Win probability 1 / (10^(-elo_diff / 400) + 1), margin estimate elo_diff / 25, simulated margins drawn rnorm(mean = estimate, sd = 13) rounded away zero. Ties possible regular season ; simulated postseason tie re-decided win probability 3-point margin. nflseedR's rest-day adjustment dropped (rest data CFB schema).

title: Compute CFB Game Results in Season Simulations — cfbseedR_compute_results
code: games <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "toy_games.csv", package = "cfbseedR")) teams <- read.csv(system.file("extdata", "toy_teams.csv", package = "cfbseedR")) games$result[games$week >= 3] <- NA teams$sim <- 1 games$sim <- 1 out <- cfbseedR_compute_results(teams, games, week_num = 3) out$games[out$games$week == 3, ] #>    sim week game_type home_team away_team result neutral #> 9    1    3       REG        A1        A4     24       0 #> 10   1    3       REG        A2        A3    -15       0 #> 11   1    3       REG        B1        B3     17       0 #> 12   1    3       REG        B2        B4     -3       0

what: Verify a Custom CFB Result Simulation Function — simulations_verify_fct
title: Verify a Custom CFB Result Simulation Function — simulations_verify_fct
text: cfbseedR supports custom functions compute results season simulations compute_results argument cfb_simulations(). function verifies custom function behaves simulator expects, mirroring nflseedR's simulations_verify_fct(). checks output structure whether game results changed expected, errors hint first problem found.

title: Verify a Custom CFB Result Simulation Function — simulations_verify_fct
code: simulations_verify_fct(compute_results, ..., games = NULL, teams = NULL)

title: Verify a Custom CFB Result Simulation Function — simulations_verify_fct
text: compute_results function compute results games. Required arguments: teams, games, week_num. must return list(teams = teams, games = games) without removing rows columns games, must fill result exactly games week == week_num & .na(result), must modify result, must produce ties (result == 0) outside regular season. ... arguments passed compute_results. games schedule results missing. Defaults bundled toy season results week 3 onwards blanked. teams teams table (team, conference, optionally sim). Defaults bundled toy teams.

title: Verify a Custom CFB Result Simulation Function — simulations_verify_fct
text: Returns TRUE invisibly problems found.

title: Verify a Custom CFB Result Simulation Function — simulations_verify_fct
code: # \donttest{ simulations_verify_fct(cfbseedR_compute_results) # }

title: cfbseedR 0.1.0
text: Initial release. cfb_standings() computes overall conference standings documented generic tiebreaker cascade (win pct, head--head, common opponents, SOV, SOS, point differential, coin flip) gated tiebreaker_depth. cfb_playoff_seeds() implements 12-team College Football Playoff straight seeding (5 highest-ranked conference champions guaranteed). cfb_simulations() simulates seasons week week pluggable compute_results function returns classed cfbseedR_simulation list standings, games, aggregated probabilities. cfbseedR_compute_results() default ELO-based results generator, faithful adaptation nflseedR_compute_results(). cfb_games_from_schedule() maps cfbfastR::load_cfb_schedules() output engine games schema. simulations_verify_fct() verifies custom compute_results functions. Adapted nflseedR (MIT) Sebastian Carl Lee Sharpe.
